Initial Demonstration: Provide agents with examples of appropriate physical interventions that lead to positive outcomes.
Reward System: Assign points as rewards for correct and effective physical interventions, encouraging agents to learn which actions yield success.
Trial and Error: Allow agents to experiment with different interventions, receiving immediate feedback (points) based on their actions.
Efficiency in Learning: Reinforcement learning allows agents to learn optimal intervention behaviors through direct feedback, accelerating their mastery.
